headword.purpose: The looked-up word. The single serif element per card; carries a pine→cranberry underline swatch.

body.purpose: Definition content (IPA, POS, learner definition, translation, example). Native system sans, capped under 75ch by the 420px card width.

brand-label.purpose: 'AI Dictionary' in the card bar, paired with the holly mark. Pine, 700.

trigger-label.purpose: The word 'Define' in the floating trigger pill.

footer-label.purpose: 'Stays on your device' privacy line beside the shield.

shadows.name: card-lift-light
shadows.purpose: Lookup card resting shadow, light mode. Three warm-tinted layers.

shadows.name: card-lift-dark
shadows.purpose: Lookup card shadow, dark mode. Deeper near-black layers to separate from a dark host page.

shadows.name: trigger-lift
shadows.purpose: Floating 'Define' pill, two warm layers, lighter than the card.

shadows.name: glow-light
shadows.purpose: Candlelight: honey-amber radial wash behind the card surface top edge (light).

shadows.name: scrim
shadows.purpose: Warm dim behind the bottom sheet on mobile. Warm-tinted, never neutral black.

motion.name: sheet-slide
motion.purpose: Bottom-sheet slide-up via transform. Ease-out, no bounce. Removed under prefers-reduced-motion.

motion.name: spin
motion.purpose: Loading spinner: a ring with a honey-amber leading arc. Used in both the trigger and the card.

components.name: Floating Trigger Pill
components.description: The 'Define' pill that appears next to a text selection. Shortest path from selection to meaning.
components.css: .ds-trigger { --ad-pine: oklch(0.46 0.075 155); --ad-cranberry: oklch(0.52 0.16 25); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; font: 600 13px/1 system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", sans-serif; color: oklch(0.31 0.025 50); background: oklch(0.985 0.009 80); border: 1px solid oklch(0.9 0.014 74); padding: 7px 12px 7px 9px; border-radius: 999px; box-shadow: 0 2px 5px oklch(0.4 0.05 50 / 0.16), 0 10px 22px -10px oklch(0.4 0.06 45 / 0.4); cursor: pointer; } .ds-trigger:hover { background: oklch(0.962 0.017 72); } .ds-trigger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.61 0.13 64); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-trigger .ds-holly { width: 18px; height: 18px; flex: none; }

components.name: Lookup Card
components.description: The candlelit-margin surface: ribbon, holly brand bar, aria-live content region, privacy footer. The product's whole identity in 420px.
components.html:
AI Dictionary

serendipity

The luck of finding something good without looking for it.
Stays on your device
components.css: .ds-card { --ad-pine: oklch(0.46 0.075 155); --ad-cranberry: oklch(0.52 0.16 25); --ad-amber: oklch(0.61 0.13 64); --ad-ink: oklch(0.31 0.025 50); --ad-ink-soft: oklch(0.45 0.022 55); --ad-line: oklch(0.9 0.014 74); box-sizing: border-box; width: 100%; max-width: 420px; margin: 0 auto; font: 15px/1.6 system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", sans-serif; color: var(--ad-ink); background: radial-gradient(125% 80% at 50% -14%, oklch(0.85 0.12 78 / 0.72), transparent 72%), oklch(0.985 0.009 80); border-radius: 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 1px oklch(0.4 0.05 50 / 0.05), 0 12px 26px -8px oklch(0.42 0.06 45 / 0.26), 0 30px 60px -24px oklch(0.4 0.06 45 / 0.3); overflow: hidden; } .ds-card .ds-ribbon { height: 4px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--ad-pine), var(--ad-amber) 52%, var(--ad-cranberry)); } .ds-card .ds-bar { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 8px; padding: 11px 12px 2px 16px; } .ds-card .ds-brand { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: .01em; color: var(--ad-pine); } .ds-card .ds-holly { width: 21px; height: 21px; flex: none; } .ds-card .ds-act { display: inline-grid; place-items: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; border: 0; background: transparent; color: var(--ad-ink-soft); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-card .ds-act:hover { background: oklch(0.962 0.017 72); color: var(--ad-ink); } .ds-card .ds-act:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ad-amber); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-card .ds-act svg { width: 15px; height: 15px; } .ds-card .ds-region { padding: 2px 16px; } .ds-card .ds-headword { font-family: Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif; font-size: 1.7rem; line-height: 1.15; letter-spacing: -.01em; margin: .1em 0 .4em; color: var(--ad-ink); display: inline-block; padding-bottom: 5px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--ad-pine), var(--ad-cranberry)) left bottom/44px 3px no-repeat; } .ds-card .ds-footer { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; margin: 8px 16px 0; padding: 10px 0 13px; border-top: 1px solid var(--ad-line); font-size: 11px; color: var(--ad-ink-soft); } .ds-card .ds-footer svg { width: 13px; height: 13px; flex: none; }

components.name: Card Action Button
components.description: The 28px icon button in the card bar (close). Quiet at rest, warm on hover.
components.html: <button class="ds-act-standalone" type="button" aria-label="Close"><svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 4L12 12M12 4L4 12"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-act-standalone { display: inline-grid; place-items: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; border: 0; background: transparent; color: oklch(0.45 0.022 55); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-act-standalone:hover { background: oklch(0.962 0.017 72); color: oklch(0.31 0.025 50); } .ds-act-standalone:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.61 0.13 64); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-act-standalone svg { width: 15px; height: 15px; }

components.name: Loading Spinner
components.description: Honey-amber leading-arc ring on a line-colored track. Appears in both the trigger and the card loading state, paired with a visually-hidden 'Looking up…' label.
components.css: @keyframes ds-spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg); } } .ds-spinner { display: inline-block; width: 18px; height: 18px; border: 2px solid oklch(0.9 0.014 74); border-top-color: oklch(0.61 0.13 64); border-radius: 50%; animation: ds-spin .7s linear infinite; vertical-align: -3px; }

narrative.overview: AI Dictionary is a warm pool of light cast in the margin of whatever you happen to be reading. A reader hits an unfamiliar English word mid-sentence; one selection brings up a small, self-owned card that explains the word in this sentence, in their language, then gets out of the way. The whole system is built to feel like a candle set down beside the page: honey-amber glow at the top of the surface, a serif headword that reads like a printed dictionary entry, and just enough festive trim (a holly mark, a thin ribbon) to make the card unmistakably ours the instant it appears over any website. The card overlays arbitrary, unpredictable pages, so it is built as a fully opaque, fully self-contained shadow-DOM surface that carries its own tokens and never leans on the host background. The warmth is the felt emotion the product designs for: the relief of understanding without friction, the calm of a tool that respects you. The cozy-Christmas identity is the carrier of that warmth, not a costume on top of it.
keyCharacteristics: In-page, not in-app. The cozy identity belongs to the lookup card that floats over the host page; browser-chrome surfaces (the options page) stay deliberately neutral.
Candlelit warmth. A honey-amber radial glow tops the card surface; the palette sits in warm OKLCH hues (45–80°) with festive accents at pine green and cranberry red.
Opaque and self-contained. Own shadow root, own tokens, full opacity. The card never borrows the page's background or fights its content.
Bilingual clarity first. The English headword and its translation are each unmistakable and scannable; meaning is never carried by color alone.
Adapts to the page. Light and dark token sets switch on prefers-color-scheme.
Privacy you can feel. A shield-marked 'Stays on your device' line is part of the card itself.

rules.name: The Trim Rule
rules.body: The festive accents (pine, cranberry) live only at the trim of the card: the 4px ribbon, the holly mark, the brand label, the headword underline. They never fill a surface or tint body text. Strip the trim away and the card is still a calm, legible reading surface.

rules.name: The Candle Rule
rules.body: Honey-amber is the only color that glows. It is the radial wash at the top of the card and the focus ring; it is never used for plain text or borders. Its job is light, not labeling.

rules.name: The No-Color-Only Rule
rules.body: The English term and its translation must be distinguishable without color (order, label, and weight carry the distinction). Color is decoration, never the signal.

rules.name: The One Serif Rule
rules.body: Georgia appears exactly once per card: the headword. Everything else is the system sans.

rules.name: The Native Sans Rule
rules.body: The body face is always the host system's UI font (system-ui stack), never a webfont.

rules.name: The One Surface Rule
rules.body: Only one cozy surface is ever visible. The card owns the bg/radius/shadow; any container around it (the bottom sheet) is transparent. Never nest the card inside a second visible card.

rules.name: The Warm Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows are tinted toward warm hue (45–52°), never neutral gray.

dos: Do keep the festive accents (pine, cranberry) at the trim only — ribbon, holly, brand label, headword underline.
Do use honey-amber as the one glowing color: the radial card glow, the focus ring, the spinner arc.
Do render every color in OKLCH and verify ≥4.5:1 body / ≥3:1 UI contrast in-browser, in both light and dark.
Do keep the card fully opaque and self-contained (own shadow root, own tokens).
Do isolate any over-page host with all: initial plus the cozy --ad-* tokens and lift it with z-index: 2147483647.
Do keep exactly one visible cozy surface: the card carries bg/radius/shadow; the bottom sheet stays transparent.
Do keep the headword the only serif and the body the native system-ui sans.
Do distinguish the two languages by order, label, and weight — never by color alone.
Do tint shadows and the scrim toward warm hue (45–52°), never neutral gray.
donts: Don't let the design feel like an ad-cluttered dictionary site — no pop-overs, no 'related searches,' no SEO sludge.
Don't grow it into a heavy SaaS dashboard — no persistent sidebars, no card grids everywhere, no settings sprawl.
Don't make it feel like it phones home — no telemetry chrome, no account walls, no 'engagement' surfaces.
Don't reach for playful AI gimmicks — no mascots, no emoji-soaked copy, no gradient-glow '✨AI✨' novelty, no sparkles.
Don't fill a surface or tint body text with pine or cranberry. They are trim, not paint.
Don't nest the card inside a second visible card or frame.
Don't introduce a third font family or use the serif anywhere but the headword.
Don't use a webfont for body — the native system sans is the rule.
Don't apply the cozy --ad-* palette to the settings/options form; browser-chrome surfaces stay deliberately neutral.
Don't use a hand-drawn / feTurbulence 'sketchy' holly — the mark is clean geometric SVG with token-bound fills.
Don't use neutral-gray shadows under the warm card; they read cold and break the candlelight.
